heading: Transfer to corporation controlled by transferor
label: 351 Transfer to corporation controlled by transferor
notes: Amendments2005—Subsec. (g)(3)(A). inserted at end “If there is not a real and meaningful likelihood that dividends beyond any limitation or preference will actually be paid, the possibility of such payments will be disregarded in determining whether stock is limited and preferred as to dividends.”Pub. L. 109–1352004—Subsec. (g)(3)(A). inserted at end “Stock shall not be treated as participating in corporate growth to any significant extent unless there is a real and meaningful likelihood of the shareholder actually participating in the earnings and growth of the corporation.”Pub. L. 108–3572002—Subsec. (h)(1). inserted comma after “liability”.Pub. L. 107–1471999—Subsec. (h)(1). struck out “, or acquires property subject to a liability,” after “liability”.Pub. L. 106–361998—Subsec. (c). , reenacted heading without change and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “In determining control for purposes of this section—Pub. L. 105–206, § 6010(c)(3)(A)“(1) the fact that any corporate transferor distributes part or all of the stock in the corporation which it receives in the exchange to its shareholders shall not be taken into account, and“(2) if the requirements of section 355 are met with respect to such distribution, the shareholders shall be treated as in control of such corporation immediately after the exchange if the shareholders own (immediately after the distribution) stock possessing—“(A) more than 50 percent of the total combined voting power of all classes of stock of such corporation entitled to vote, and“(B) more than 50 percent of the total value of shares of all classes of stock of such corporation.”Subsec. (c)(2). inserted “, or the fact that the corporation whose stock was distributed issues additional stock,” after “dispose of part or all of the distributed stock”.Pub. L. 105–277Subsec. (g)(1)(A) to (C). , inserted “and” at end of subpar. (A), added subpar. (B), and struck out former subpars. (B) and (C) which read as follows:Pub. L. 105–206, § 6010(e)(1)“(B) subsection (b) shall apply to such transferor, and“(C) such nonqualified preferred stock shall be treated as other property for purposes of applying subsection (b).”1997—Subsec. (c). , amended heading and text of subsec. (c) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “In determining control, for purposes of this section, the fact that any corporate transferor distributes part or all of the stock which it receives in the exchange to its shareholders shall not be taken into account.”Pub. (b), (d), (e)(2). , struck out “or securities” after “stock”.Pub. L. 101–239, § 7203(b)(1)Subsec. (g)(2). , substituted “stock or property” for “stock, securities, or property”.Pub. L. 105–34, title X, § 1012(d)111 Stat. 917Pub. L. 105–206, title VI, § 6010(c)(1)112 Stat. 813Aug. 5, 1997July 22, 1998“(1).—Section 355 rulesThe amendments made by subsections (a) and (b) [amending sections 355 and 358 of this title] shall apply to distributions after ; except that the amendment made by subsection (a) [amending ] shall apply to such distributions only if pursuant to a plan (or series of related transactions) which involves an acquisition described in section 355(e)(2)(A)(ii) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 occurring after such date.April 16, 1997section 355 of this title“(2).—Divisive transactionsThe amendments made by subsection (c) [amending this section and ] shall apply to transfers after the date of the enactment of this Act [].section 368 of this titleAug. 5, 1997“(3).—Transition ruleThe amendments made by this section [amending this section and sections 355, 358, and 368 of this title] shall not apply to any distribution pursuant to a plan (or series of related transactions) which involves an acquisition described in section 355(e)(2)(A)(ii) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (or, in the case of the amendments made by subsection (c), any transfer) occurring after , if such acquisition or transfer is—April 16, 1997“(A)made pursuant to an agreement which was binding on such date and at all times thereafter,“(B)described in a ruling request submitted to the Internal Revenue Service on or before such date, or“(C)described on or before such date in a public announcement or in a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission required solely by reason of the acquisition or transfer.This paragraph shall not apply to any agreement, ruling request, or public announcement or filing unless it identifies the acquirer of the distributing corporation or any controlled corporation, or the transferee, whichever is applicable.”, , , provided that:Pub. children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: No gain or loss shall be recognized if property is transferred to a corporation by one or more persons solely in exchange for stock in such corporation and immediately after the exchange such person or persons are in control (as defined in section 368(c)) of the corporation.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount of money received, plus

children.label: B
children.text: the fair market value of such other property received; and
no loss to such recipient shall be recognized.

children.heading: Special rules where distribution to shareholders
children.label: c Special rules where distribution to shareholders

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In determining control for purposes of this section, the fact that any corporate transferor distributes part or all of the stock in the corporation which it receives in the exchange to its shareholders shall not be taken into account.

children.heading: Special rule for section 355
children.label: 2 Special rule for section 355
children.text: If the requirements of section 355 (or so much of section 356 as relates to section 355) are met with respect to a distribution described in paragraph (1), then, solely for purposes of determining the tax treatment of the transfers of property to the controlled corporation by the distributing corporation, the fact that the shareholders of the distributing corporation dispose of part or all of the distributed stock, or the fact that the corporation whose stock was distributed issues additional stock, shall not be taken into account in determining control for purposes of this section.

children.heading: Services, certain indebtedness, and accrued interest not treated as property
children.label: d Services, certain indebtedness, and accrued interest not treated as property

children.text: services,
indebtedness of the transferee corporation which is not evidenced by a security, or
interest on indebtedness of the transferee corporation which accrued on or after the beginning of the transferor’s holding period for the debt,

children.heading: Transfer of property to an investment company
children.label: 1 Transfer of property to an investment company
A
children.text: by taking into account all stock and securities held by the company, and

children.label: i
children.text: money,

children.label: ii
children.text: stocks and other equity interests in a corporation, evidences of indebtedness, options, forward or futures contracts, notional principal contracts and derivatives,

children.label: iii
children.text: any foreign currency,

children.label: iv
children.text: any interest in a real estate investment trust, a common trust fund, a regulated investment company, a publicly-traded partnership (as defined in section 7704(b)) or any other equity interest (other than in a corporation) which pursuant to its terms or any other arrangement is readily convertible into, or exchangeable for, any asset described in any preceding clause, this clause or clause (v) or (viii),

children.label: v
children.text: except to the extent provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary, any interest in a precious metal, unless such metal is used or held in the active conduct of a trade or business after the contribution,

children.label: vi
children.text: except as otherwise provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary, interests in any entity if substantially all of the assets of such entity consist (directly or indirectly) of any assets described in any preceding clause or clause (viii),

children.label: vii
children.text: to the extent provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary, any interest in any entity not described in clause (vi), but only to the extent of the value of such interest that is attributable to assets listed in clauses (i) through (v) or clause (viii), or

children.label: viii
children.text: any other asset specified in regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Title 11 or similar case
children.label: 2 Title 11 or similar case
children.text: A transfer of property of a debtor pursuant to a plan while the debtor is under the jurisdiction of a court in a title 11 or similar case (within the meaning of section 368(a)(3)(A)), to the extent that the stock received in the exchange is used to satisfy the indebtedness of such debtor.

property is transferred to a corporation (hereinafter in this subsection referred to as the “controlled corporation”) in an exchange with respect to which gain or loss is not recognized (in whole or in part) to the transferor under this section, and
such exchange is not in pursuance of a plan of reorganization,

children.heading: Nonqualified preferred stock not treated as stock
children.label: g Nonqualified preferred stock not treated as stock
A
children.text: subsection (a) shall not apply to such transferor, and

children.label: i
children.text: subsection (b) shall apply to such transferor; and

children.label: ii
children.text: such nonqualified preferred stock shall be treated as other property for purposes of applying subsection (b).

children.label: i
children.text: the holder of such stock has the right to require the issuer or a related person to redeem or purchase the stock,

children.label: ii
children.text: the issuer or a related person is required to redeem or purchase such stock,

children.label: iii
children.text: the issuer or a related person has the right to redeem or purchase the stock and, as of the issue date, it is more likely than not that such right will be exercised, or

children.label: iv
children.text: the dividend rate on such stock varies in whole or in part (directly or indirectly) with reference to interest rates, commodity prices, or other similar indices.

children.heading: Limitations
children.label: B Limitations
children.text: Clauses (i), (ii), and (iii) of subparagraph (A) shall apply only if the right or obligation referred to therein may be exercised within the 20-year period beginning on the issue date of such stock and such right or obligation is not subject to a contingency which, as of the issue date, makes remote the likelihood of the redemption or purchase.

children.heading: Exceptions for certain rights or obligations
children.label: C Exceptions for certain rights or obligations
I
children.text: it may be exercised only upon the death, disability, or mental incompetency of the holder, or

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a right or obligation to redeem or purchase stock transferred in connection with the performance of services for the issuer or a related person (and which represents reasonable compensation), it may be exercised only upon the holder’s separation from service from the issuer or a related person.

children.label: I
children.text: a corporation if any class of stock in such corporation or a related party is readily tradable on an established securities market or otherwise, or

children.label: II
children.text: any other corporation if such exchange is part of a transaction or series of transactions in which such corporation is to become a corporation described in subclause (I).

children.heading: Preferred stock
children.label: A Preferred stock
children.text: The term “preferred stock” means stock which is limited and preferred as to dividends and does not participate in corporate growth to any significant extent. Stock shall not be treated as participating in corporate growth to any significant extent unless there is a real and meaningful likelihood of the shareholder actually participating in the earnings and growth of the corporation. If there is not a real and meaningful likelihood that dividends beyond any limitation or preference will actually be paid, the possibility of such payments will be disregarded in determining whether stock is limited and preferred as to dividends.

children.heading: Related person
children.label: B Related person
children.text: A person shall be treated as related to another person if they bear a relationship to such other person described in section 267(b) or 707(b).

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 4 Regulations
children.text: The Secretary may prescribe such regulations as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of this subsection and sections 354(a)(2)(C), 355(a)(3)(D), and 356(e). The Secretary may also prescribe regulations, consistent with the treatment under this subsection and such sections, for the treatment of nonqualified preferred stock under other provisions of this title.
For special rule where another party to the exchange assumes a liability, see section 357.
For the basis of stock or property received in an exchange to which this section applies, see sections 358 and 362.
For special rule in the case of an exchange described in this section but which results in a gift, see section 2501 and following.
For special rule in the case of an exchange described in this section but which has the effect of the payment of compensation by the corporation or by a transferor, see section 61(a)(1).
For coordination of this section with section 304, see section 304(b)(3).
